documents.title: University of Dayton public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: The University of Dayton frames AI through Catholic and human-centered principles, integrating AI into curriculum while leveraging UDRI applied research and workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: University of Dayton's public AI policy posture is permissive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.scope: The University of Dayton frames AI through Catholic and human-centered principles, integrating AI into curriculum while leveraging UDRI applied research and workforce preparation.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for OH. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: University of Dayton is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: The University of Dayton frames AI through Catholic and human-centered principles, integrating AI into curriculum while leveraging UDRI applied research and workforce preparation. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
